content.text: Why Prompt Changes Must Reference Their Evals

A prompt commit without a corresponding eval reference creates a dangerous asymmetry: the repository records what the model was told to do, but not whether that instruction was verified. When a production incident occurs, the on-call engineer cannot look at the commit history and immediately know which prompts were tested, what the test covered, and what the pass rate was at deploy time.

The commit message is the one place every change must pass through. Making it carry a machine-readable pointer to the eval that validated the change turns the git log into a traceability ledger: any prompt in production can be traced to the eval that certified it, and any eval result can be traced back to the prompt commit that triggered it.

This sub-subtopic defines the footer fields that embed eval references in commit messages, the tooling to extract and validate those references in CI, how to build a production audit log from the commit history, and how to query that log during an incident.
The Traceability Chain

attrs.alt: Prompt–Eval Traceability Chain: commit → prompt hash → eval reference → audit log

content.text: The chain has three links:
Commit → Eval reference
: the commit footer Eval-ref: points to the eval case file or suite that validated the change.
Commit → Prompt hash
: the commit footer Prompt-hash: records the SHA-256 hash of the prompt file at that commit, making it possible to verify that the deployed prompt matches the one the eval tested.
Both → Audit log
: a CI job extracts these footers and appends a structured record to a production audit log that operators can query without reading git.
Footer Fields for Prompt and Eval Linking
Two trailer fields are defined for commits that change the AI layer. Both use the git commit trailer format (key: value after a blank line in the body):

prompt(triage-agent): tighten refusal boundary for off-topic legal questions

The refusal threshold was too permissive: users asking for specific legal
advice were receiving partial answers rather than a referral. This commit
updates the system prompt to add an explicit instruction block for legal
and medical advice categories.

Eval-ref: evals/triage/refusal-v4.json
Prompt-hash: a3f2d9c1e7b40512
Approved-by: alice@example.com
Field definitions:
Footer Field
Format
Required For
Purpose
Eval-ref:
Path relative to repo root, or suite name
All prompt, tool, agent commits
Points to the eval case or suite that must pass for this change to be deployable.
Prompt-hash:
16-character hex (SHA-256 truncated)
All prompt commits
Snapshot of the prompt file content at commit time. Detected drift = prompt was modified after eval ran.
Approved-by:
Email or GitHub handle
Breaking changes (!)
Records who approved the change, supplementing the PR review trail.
Incident-ref:
Incident ID or ticket URL
fix commits
Links the fix to the incident it resolves. Also a search target when building incident timelines.
Computing and Validating the Prompt Hash

The Prompt-hash field must match the SHA-256 of the prompt file at the commit being authored. A pre-commit hook computes the hash and either appends it automatically or rejects the commit if it is absent:

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# .husky/prepare-commit-msg
# Appends Prompt-hash to commit messages for prompt/* commits.
# Called with the path to the commit message file as $1.

COMMIT_MSG_FILE="$1"
COMMIT_MSG=$(cat "$COMMIT_MSG_FILE")

# Only process prompt, tool, or agent commits.
if ! echo "$COMMIT_MSG" | grep -qE "^(prompt|tool|agent)\("; then
  exit 0
fi

# Find files staged in the prompts/ directory.
STAGED_PROMPT=$(git diff --cached --name-only | grep "^prompts/" | head -1)
if [ -z "$STAGED_PROMPT" ]; then
  exit 0
fi

# Compute hash from the staged content (index blob, not working tree).
HASH=$(git show ":$STAGED_PROMPT" | sha256sum | cut -c1-16)

# Append Prompt-hash footer if not already present.
if ! echo "$COMMIT_MSG" | grep -q "^Prompt-hash:"; then
  printf "\nPrompt-hash: %s\n" "$HASH" >> "$COMMIT_MSG_FILE"
fi
A CI job then verifies that the hash in the footer matches the actual file content at the commit:

# scripts/verify_prompt_hash.py
"""
For each commit in the PR range, verify that the Prompt-hash footer
matches the SHA-256 of the prompt file at that commit.

Usage:
    python scripts/verify_prompt_hash.py <base_sha> <head_sha>

Exits with code 1 if any mismatch is found, so CI fails loudly.
"""
import subprocess, hashlib, re, sys

def get_commits(base: str, head: str) -> list[str]:
    """Return list of commit SHAs between base and head (oldest first)."""
    result = subprocess.run(
        ["git", "log", "--pretty=%H", f"{base}..{head}"],
        capture_output=True, text=True, check=True,
    )
    return result.stdout.strip().splitlines()[::-1]

def get_commit_body(sha: str) -> str:
    """Return the full commit message for the given SHA."""
    result = subprocess.run(
        ["git", "show", "--no-patch", "--pretty=%B", sha],
        capture_output=True, text=True, check=True,
    )
    return result.stdout

def extract_footer(body: str, key: str) -> str | None:
    """Extract a commit trailer value by key, e.g. 'Prompt-hash'."""
    m = re.search(rf"^{re.escape(key)}:\s*(.+)$", body, re.MULTILINE)
    return m.group(1).strip() if m else None

def get_staged_file_content(sha: str, path: str) -> bytes:
    """Return the content of a file as it existed at a given commit."""
    result = subprocess.run(
        ["git", "show", f"{sha}:{path}"],
        capture_output=True, check=True,
    )
    return result.stdout

def sha256_16(content: bytes) -> str:
    """Compute SHA-256 and return the first 16 hex characters."""
    return hashlib.sha256(content).hexdigest()[:16]

def verify_pr_prompt_hashes(base: str, head: str) -> bool:
    """
    Walk commits in the PR range. For each prompt/tool/agent commit that
    has a Prompt-hash footer, verify it matches the file at that commit.

    Returns True if all hashes are valid, False if any mismatch is found.
    """
    commits = get_commits(base, head)
    all_ok = True

    for sha in commits:
        body = get_commit_body(sha)
        first_line = body.splitlines()[0] if body.strip() else ""

        # Only check commits that touch the AI layer.
        if not re.match(r"^(prompt|tool|agent)\(", first_line):
            continue

        expected_hash = extract_footer(body, "Prompt-hash")
        if not expected_hash:
            # Missing footer on a prompt commit is a warning, not an error,
            # since the hook appends it automatically — a missing hash means
            # the commit was authored outside the normal workflow.
            print(f"WARNING {sha[:8]}: prompt commit is missing Prompt-hash footer")
            continue

        # Find the first changed file in the prompts/ directory.
        changed = subprocess.run(
            ["git", "diff-tree", "--no-commit-id", "-r", "--name-only", sha],
            capture_output=True, text=True, check=True,
        ).stdout.strip().splitlines()
        prompt_files = [f for f in changed if f.startswith("prompts/")]

        for prompt_path in prompt_files:
            content = get_staged_file_content(sha, prompt_path)
            actual_hash = sha256_16(content)
            if actual_hash != expected_hash:
                print(
                    f"FAIL {sha[:8]}: Prompt-hash mismatch for {prompt_path}\n"
                    f"  footer:   {expected_hash}\n"
                    f"  computed: {actual_hash}"
                )
                all_ok = False
            else:
                print(f"OK   {sha[:8]}: {prompt_path} hash verified ({expected_hash})")

    return all_ok

if __name__ == "__main__":
    base_sha, head_sha = sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2]
    if not verify_pr_prompt_hashes(base_sha, head_sha):
        sys.exit(1)
    print("All Prompt-hash footers verified.")
Eval Reference Validation

The Eval-ref footer must point to a file that exists in the repository. If it does not, the commit is referencing an eval that was never written — which means the change was not validated. A CI check enforces this:

# scripts/verify_eval_refs.py
"""
For each AI-layer commit in the PR range, check that the Eval-ref footer
points to a file that exists at HEAD.

Usage:
    python scripts/verify_eval_refs.py <base_sha> <head_sha>

Exits with code 1 if any reference is missing or the footer is absent on
a commit that requires it.
"""
import subprocess, re, sys
from pathlib import Path

REQUIRES_EVAL_REF = {"prompt", "tool", "agent", "fix"}

def get_commits(base: str, head: str) -> list[str]:
    result = subprocess.run(
        ["git", "log", "--pretty=%H", f"{base}..{head}"],
        capture_output=True, text=True, check=True,
    )
    return result.stdout.strip().splitlines()

def get_commit_body(sha: str) -> str:
    result = subprocess.run(
        ["git", "show", "--no-patch", "--pretty=%B", sha],
        capture_output=True, text=True, check=True,
    )
    return result.stdout

def parse_type(subject: str) -> str:
    m = re.match(r"^([a-z]+)", subject)
    return m.group(1) if m else "unknown"

def verify_pr_eval_refs(base: str, head: str) -> bool:
    commits = get_commits(base, head)
    all_ok = True
    repo_root = Path(
        subprocess.run(
            ["git", "rev-parse", "--show-toplevel"],
            capture_output=True, text=True, check=True,
        ).stdout.strip()
    )

    for sha in commits:
        body = get_commit_body(sha)
        subject = body.splitlines()[0] if body.strip() else ""
        ctype = parse_type(subject)

        if ctype not in REQUIRES_EVAL_REF:
            continue

        m = re.search(r"^Eval-ref:\s*(.+)$", body, re.MULTILINE)
        if not m:
            print(f"FAIL {sha[:8]}: {ctype} commit missing Eval-ref footer: {subject!r}")
            all_ok = False
            continue

        eval_path = repo_root / m.group(1).strip()
        if not eval_path.exists():
            print(f"FAIL {sha[:8]}: Eval-ref points to missing file: {eval_path}")
            all_ok = False
        else:
            print(f"OK   {sha[:8]}: Eval-ref exists: {m.group(1).strip()}")

    return all_ok

if __name__ == "__main__":
    base_sha, head_sha = sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2]
    if not verify_pr_eval_refs(base_sha, head_sha):
        sys.exit(1)
    print("All Eval-ref footers verified.")
CI Integration
Both verification scripts run as required jobs in the pull request CI pipeline:

# .github/workflows/traceability.yml
name: Prompt–Eval Traceability

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

jobs:
  verify-prompt-hashes:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Verify Prompt-hash footers
        run: |
          python scripts/verify_prompt_hash.py \
            "${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}" \
            "${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}"

  verify-eval-refs:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Verify Eval-ref footers
        run: |
          python scripts/verify_eval_refs.py \
            "${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}" \
            "${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}"

  build-audit-entry:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [verify-prompt-hashes, verify-eval-refs]
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Build audit log entries for this PR
        run: |
          python scripts/build_audit_entries.py \
            "${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}" \
            "${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}" \
            >> audit_preview.jsonl

      - name: Upload audit preview as artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: audit-entries
          path: audit_preview.jsonl
Building the Production Audit Log
After each deploy, a script extracts the traceability footers from the commits that went out and appends records to a centralised audit log. This log is the primary tool for incident investigation:

# scripts/build_audit_entries.py
"""
Extract traceability data from commits in a range and emit
JSONL audit log entries, one per AI-layer commit.

Usage:
    python scripts/build_audit_entries.py <base_sha> <head_sha>

Output is written to stdout as JSONL so the caller can redirect
it to an audit file or send it to a log ingestion endpoint.
"""
import subprocess, re, json, sys
from datetime import datetime, timezone

AI_LAYER_TYPES = {"prompt", "tool", "agent", "eval", "fix", "feat"}

def get_commits(base: str, head: str) -> list[dict]:
    """Return a list of dicts with sha, subject, body, author, and timestamp."""
    fmt = "%H%x00%s%x00%ae%x00%aI"
    result = subprocess.run(
        ["git", "log", f"--pretty={fmt}", f"{base}..{head}"],
        capture_output=True, text=True, check=True,
    )
    commits = []
    for line in result.stdout.strip().splitlines():
        parts = line.split("\x00")
        if len(parts) < 4:
            continue
        sha, subject, author, timestamp = parts
        body = subprocess.run(
            ["git", "show", "--no-patch", "--pretty=%B", sha],
            capture_output=True, text=True, check=True,
        ).stdout
        commits.append({
            "sha": sha,
            "subject": subject,
            "body": body,
            "author": author,
            "commit_timestamp": timestamp,
        })
    return commits

def extract_trailer(body: str, key: str) -> str | None:
    m = re.search(rf"^{re.escape(key)}:\s*(.+)$", body, re.MULTILINE)
    return m.group(1).strip() if m else None

def parse_type_scope(subject: str) -> tuple[str, str, bool]:
    m = re.match(r"^([a-z]+)(?:\(([^)]+)\))?(!)?: ", subject)
    if not m:
        return "unknown", "", False
    return m.group(1), m.group(2) or "", m.group(3) == "!"

def build_audit_entries(base: str, head: str) -> list[dict]:
    """
    Walk the commit range and build one audit entry per AI-layer commit.
    Each entry captures everything needed for incident investigation:
    the commit identity, the prompt hash, the eval reference, and the author.
    """
    commits = get_commits(base, head)
    entries = []

    for c in commits:
        ctype, scope, breaking = parse_type_scope(c["subject"])
        if ctype not in AI_LAYER_TYPES:
            continue

        entry = {
            "git_sha":          c["sha"],
            "commit_type":      ctype,
            "scope":            scope,
            "breaking":         breaking,
            "subject":          c["subject"],
            "author":           c["author"],
            "commit_timestamp": c["commit_timestamp"],
            "prompt_hash":      extract_trailer(c["body"], "Prompt-hash"),
            "eval_ref":         extract_trailer(c["body"], "Eval-ref"),
            "incident_ref":     extract_trailer(c["body"], "Incident-ref"),
            "approved_by":      extract_trailer(c["body"], "Approved-by"),
            "extracted_at":     datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        }
        entries.append(entry)

    return entries

if __name__ == "__main__":
    base_sha, head_sha = sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2]
    for entry in build_audit_entries(base_sha, head_sha):
        print(json.dumps(entry))
Sample output for a deployment that included two prompt commits and a fix:
Querying the Audit Log During an Incident
The JSONL format makes the audit log queryable with standard tools. Common incident investigation queries:

# 1. Find every prompt change to triage-agent in the last 30 deployments
cat production_audit.jsonl | \
  jq 'select(.scope == "triage-agent" and .commit_type == "prompt")' | \
  jq '{sha: .git_sha[0:8], subject: .subject, hash: .prompt_hash, eval: .eval_ref}'

# 2. Find all breaking changes since a specific date
cat production_audit.jsonl | \
  jq 'select(.breaking == true and .commit_timestamp > "2025-06-01T00:00:00")' | \
  jq '{sha: .git_sha[0:8], subject: .subject, approved_by: .approved_by}'

# 3. Find all commits that reference a specific eval file
cat production_audit.jsonl | \
  jq 'select(.eval_ref == "evals/triage/refusal-v4.json")' | \
  jq '{sha: .git_sha[0:8], subject: .subject, hash: .prompt_hash}'

# 4. Find the prompt hash currently in production for triage-agent
cat production_audit.jsonl | \
  jq 'select(.scope == "triage-agent" and .commit_type == "prompt")' | \
  tail -1 | jq .prompt_hash

# 5. Trace an incident back to a prompt commit
INCIDENT_REF="INC-2025-0612"
cat production_audit.jsonl | \
  jq --arg ref "$INCIDENT_REF" 'select(.incident_ref == $ref)'

Query 4 is particularly valuable during an incident: it gives you the prompt hash that is currently serving traffic, which you can verify against the prompt file in the repository and against the eval that certified it:

# Verify the deployed prompt hash matches the repo file
DEPLOYED_HASH=$(cat production_audit.jsonl | \
  jq -r 'select(.scope == "triage-agent" and .commit_type == "prompt")' | \
  tail -1 | jq -r .prompt_hash)

REPO_HASH=$(sha256sum prompts/triage-agent/system_prompt.json | cut -c1-16)

if [ "$DEPLOYED_HASH" = "$REPO_HASH" ]; then
  echo "Prompt matches repository — no drift detected."
else
  echo "DRIFT DETECTED: deployed=$DEPLOYED_HASH repo=$REPO_HASH"
  echo "The prompt in production does not match the current repository state."
fi
Testing the Traceability Infrastructure

# tests/test_traceability.py
"""Unit tests for commit footer extraction and audit entry building."""
import unittest
from unittest.mock import patch
from scripts.build_audit_entries import (
    parse_type_scope, extract_trailer, build_audit_entries,
)

SAMPLE_BODY = """prompt(triage-agent): tighten refusal boundary

The refusal threshold was too permissive for legal/medical questions.

Eval-ref: evals/triage/refusal-v4.json
Prompt-hash: a3f2d9c1e7b40512
Approved-by: alice@example.com
"""

class TestParseTypeScope(unittest.TestCase):

    def test_prompt_with_scope(self):
        ctype, scope, breaking = parse_type_scope(
            "prompt(triage-agent): tighten refusal boundary"
        )
        self.assertEqual(ctype, "prompt")
        self.assertEqual(scope, "triage-agent")
        self.assertFalse(breaking)

    def test_breaking_tool(self):
        ctype, scope, breaking = parse_type_scope(
            "tool(tool-schema)!: rename intent to intent_type"
        )
        self.assertEqual(ctype, "tool")
        self.assertTrue(breaking)

    def test_chore_no_scope(self):
        ctype, scope, breaking = parse_type_scope("chore: upgrade Node to 22.x")
        self.assertEqual(ctype, "chore")
        self.assertEqual(scope, "")
        self.assertFalse(breaking)

class TestExtractTrailer(unittest.TestCase):

    def test_extracts_eval_ref(self):
        ref = extract_trailer(SAMPLE_BODY, "Eval-ref")
        self.assertEqual(ref, "evals/triage/refusal-v4.json")

    def test_extracts_prompt_hash(self):
        h = extract_trailer(SAMPLE_BODY, "Prompt-hash")
        self.assertEqual(h, "a3f2d9c1e7b40512")

    def test_extracts_approved_by(self):
        a = extract_trailer(SAMPLE_BODY, "Approved-by")
        self.assertEqual(a, "alice@example.com")

    def test_missing_trailer_returns_none(self):
        result = extract_trailer(SAMPLE_BODY, "Incident-ref")
        self.assertIsNone(result)

class TestBuildAuditEntries(unittest.TestCase):

    def _mock_commits(self, commits):
        return patch("scripts.build_audit_entries.get_commits", return_value=commits)

    def test_prompt_commit_produces_entry(self):
        commits = [{
            "sha": "abc123",
            "subject": "prompt(triage-agent): tighten refusal boundary",
            "body": SAMPLE_BODY,
            "author": "alice@example.com",
            "commit_timestamp": "2025-06-14T10:15:00+00:00",
        }]
        with self._mock_commits(commits):
            entries = build_audit_entries("base", "head")
        self.assertEqual(len(entries), 1)
        e = entries[0]
        self.assertEqual(e["commit_type"], "prompt")
        self.assertEqual(e["scope"], "triage-agent")
        self.assertEqual(e["prompt_hash"], "a3f2d9c1e7b40512")
        self.assertEqual(e["eval_ref"], "evals/triage/refusal-v4.json")

    def test_chore_commit_is_excluded(self):
        commits = [{
            "sha": "xyz789",
            "subject": "chore: upgrade Node to 22.x",
            "body": "chore: upgrade Node to 22.x\n",
            "author": "bob@example.com",
            "commit_timestamp": "2025-06-14T10:20:00+00:00",
        }]
        with self._mock_commits(commits):
            entries = build_audit_entries("base", "head")
        self.assertEqual(len(entries), 0)

    def test_breaking_change_flagged(self):
        commits = [{
            "sha": "brk001",
            "subject": "prompt(triage-agent)!: switch response format to JSON",
            "body": (
                "prompt(triage-agent)!: switch response format to JSON\n\n"
                "BREAKING CHANGE: output is now JSON.\n\n"
                "Eval-ref: evals/triage/json-format-v1.json\n"
                "Prompt-hash: b7e2f3c1a4d80921\n"
                "Approved-by: carol@example.com\n"
            ),
            "author": "carol@example.com",
            "commit_timestamp": "2025-06-14T10:25:00+00:00",
        }]
        with self._mock_commits(commits):
            entries = build_audit_entries("base", "head")
        self.assertTrue(entries[0]["breaking"])
        self.assertEqual(entries[0]["approved_by"], "carol@example.com")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()
Summary

Commit messages that link prompt changes to their eval results create a complete traceability chain: from the instruction sent to the model, through the hash that identifies the exact prompt content, to the eval file that certified the change was safe to deploy. Two structured footer fields — Eval-ref and Prompt-hash — carry this linkage in a machine-readable form that CI can validate on every pull request. The resulting JSONL audit log becomes the primary tool for incident investigation, making it possible to answer, from a single file query, which prompt was serving traffic at any given time, which eval certified it, and who approved the change.
